🎵 440 vs 432 Hz · MYTH OR REALITY? ✨

432 Hz and 440 Hz are central tuning frequencies used to organize and tune musical systems 🎼

For centuries, there was no official universal tuning standard.
Each region and orchestra tuned instruments differently — many of them around 432 Hz 🌍🎻

It wasn’t until 1939 that A=440 Hz became the global standard, mainly for practical reasons like unifying orchestras, instrument production, and musical consistency 🎹

After that, countless theories began to appear:
That 440 Hz was “unnatural,” manipulated emotions, caused stress or anxiety, or was even promoted to control human consciousness 🧠⚡

But reality is both simpler… and more interesting. 🔬

There is no scientifically proven “good” or “bad” frequency ❌✅
However, different frequencies can create noticeably different vibration patterns, resonance, and sound experiences 🌊🎶

What makes this topic fascinating is the power people associate with sound and frequency.

Personally, I like letting the frequencies “speak for themselves.”
And through cymatics, they can do exactly that 🔊✨

👉 Watch the video carefully and notice the visual differences between both frequencies.

💠 In 432 Hz, the patterns often appear more fluid, organic, dynamic, and harmonious.
That’s one reason why many people use it in sound therapy and meditation practices ⚕️🌀

Next week we begin the VOZ VIVA Laboratory — a space to explore frequencies through the human voice and understand how vibration can organize (or disorganize) our internal systems 🎤🌌

🙋🏽‍♀️ If you’d like to learn more, message me and I’ll send you all the information.

The video belongs to an amazing creator ✨ #frequency #life #vibration

Some musicians stick with 440 Hz because it’s the established standard, but others musicians experiment with 432 Hz to explore different emotional effects and sounds.

1. Reduces stress and anxiety
The soothing nature of 432 Hz may help decrease the body’s production of the stress hormone cortisol, making it easier for listeners to relax and feel less overwhelmed in stressful situations.

2. Enhances mental clarity and focus
Many users report that this tuning helps them think more clearly and concentrate better. This can be particularly beneficial in work or study environments where focus is important.

3. Promotes healing and pain relief
There are claims that 432 Hz music can promote physical healing and reduce pain, though more rigorous scientific research is needed to prove this definitively.

4. Deepens emotional connection
Some people say that music at 432 Hz is more comforting than music tuned to 440 Hz, which allows them to form a deeper emotional connection to the music. This can be particularly meaningful in music therapy.

5.Improves listening experience
Some audiophiles and music enthusiasts believe that 432 Hz tuning improves the listening experience by producing a more balanced, clear, and harmonious sound. This quality can make music feel more pleasant and enjoyable, potentially increasing listeners’ overall satisfaction with their music choices.  JAH

Japan is turning footsteps into electricity using piezoelectric tiles. Every step creates a small amount of energy, and millions of steps together power LED lights and digital systems-supporting a more sustainable urban future.⚡✨

Writing
🐦 Ever wondered how people create realistic bird sounds using only their hands? It’s a fun skill that turns your hands into a natural whistle! Simply cup your hands tightly together, leaving a small gap between your thumbs. Place your lips against the thumb opening and blow a steady stream of air across the gap. Adjust the size of the opening and the strength of your breath until you hear a clear whistle. With practice, you can create chirps, tweets, and even owl-like calls that sound surprisingly real! The secret is maintaining an airtight seal and experimenting with airflow and hand position. 🎶🌿

The Core Technology — Hemi-Sync
This is the engine of the entire program.
How It Works:
You hear a slightly different frequency in each ear through headphones
Example: 200 Hz in the left ear, 210 Hz in the right ear
Your brain automatically generates a third tone — the difference between them (10 Hz)
This is called a binaural beat
That 10 Hz tone doesn't exist in the room — it's created inside your brain
This forces the left and right hemispheres to synchronize
Why That Matters:
Normal waking consciousness = both hemispheres operating independently and asynchronously
Gateway state = both hemispheres locked in perfect coherence
The document describes this as creating a state where:
The brain operates more like a holistic resonance system than a fragmented processing unit
This coherence is measurable on EEG — it's not theoretical.

Everything in this reality is vibrating energy at different frequencies. Quantum physics has proven that at the most fundamental level, everything we perceive as solid is actually made up of subatomic particles that are constantly moving, shifting, and responding to the energy around them. What we call “matter” is just energy vibrating at a slow enough frequency to be perceived by our senses. This means that nothing is truly fixed, everything is in a state of possibility, waiting to be shaped by our consciousness.

Your thoughts are electric, and your feelings are magnetic. Every thought you think sends out an electrical signal, and every feeling you hold generates a magnetic field. Together, these create your vibrational frequency, which dictates what you attract into your reality. You are like a broadcasting tower, constantly sending out energetic signals that call experiences, people, and opportunities into your life.

This is where the quantum field comes in. Scientists have discovered that particles exist in a state of pure potential until they are observed. This is known as the observer effect, particles remain as waves of possibility until consciousness collapses them into form. In other words, you are literally shaping reality by where you place your focus, attention, and energy.

Your external world is not happening to you; it is happening because of you. Every belief, every expectation, every emotional state is influencing the quantum field, collapsing particles into the experiences that match your dominant vibration. If you live in fear, lack, or doubt, you will continue to see evidence of those frequencies reflected back to you. If you shift into abundance, joy, and certainty, your reality will rearrange itself accordingly.

You are not just a participant in this reality, you are the creator of it. The key is to consciously align your thoughts and emotions with the frequency of what you desire. The more you embody the feelings of already having what you want, the more you magnetize those experiences into your life. Reality is responding to you in every moment. The question is: What are you broadcasting? What frequency are you tuning into?

Listening to music tuned to 432 Hz is believed to have the ability to destroy negative energy and raise positive vibrations within the listener. It is said that this frequency is in alignment with the natural vibrations of the universe, promoting feelings of peace, harmony, and healing.

In addition to promoting inner peace, listening to music at 432 Hz is also believed to help release blockages in the body's energy field, allowing for the free flow of energy and promoting overall well-being. Some people even use this frequency as a form of meditation, using it to clear their mind and connect with their higher self.

🔊 What if the music you’ve listened to your entire life was intentionally tuned away from a more natural frequency?

For decades, debate has surrounded the shift from 432Hz to 440Hz.

Some researchers and sound enthusiasts claim that:
✨ 432Hz aligns more closely with patterns found in nature.
✨ It creates a more relaxing listening experience.
✨ Ancient instruments and classical compositions were often closer to this tuning.

Others argue that there is no solid scientific evidence proving 432Hz has unique healing properties over 440Hz.

So what’s the truth?

The answer may lie somewhere between science, psychology, and personal experience.

🎧 Listen to both and decide for yourself.

This 40Hz pulsed frequency can reduce toxic plaque in your brain 🤯 (see below on how to listen to a longer audio 👇) 

Amyloid-beta plaques can start building up in your brain as early as your mid-20s, especially if you're genetically predisposed to Alzheimer's or regularly experience chronic stress, poor diet, and lack of sleep‼️ As we get older, around our 30s-40s, our brain’s ability to clear out these plaques starts to slow down, with a bigger drop in the 50s-60s.

These plaques mess with how your brain cells communicate, cause mood changes, and can trigger inflammation and oxidative stress that actually damage brain cells. Over time, they may even contribute to Alzheimer's disease.

These studies also show that when you add 40Hz flickering light to the sound, it helps clear plaque even more.

👉 To find this frequency for free, search for “isochronic 40Hz pulse” on YouTube (this is the same sound that was used in the MIT study)

They hide this secret experiment 👇

First, scientists placed a sample of water under a microscope and studied its molecular structure.
Then they played a specific sound — a frequency of 440 Hz, which is what almost all modern music uses today.

Next, they played another sound — 432 Hz.

What they discovered changed everything:
The 440 Hz frequency created chaotic, disconnected patterns,
while the 432 Hz frequency formed beautiful, harmonious shapes — like sacred geometry itself.

Your body is over 70% water, so if frequencies can change the structure of water…

When You Raise Your Spiritual Vibration You Attract And Manifest Your Heart Desires.💫🤎
 
It's all about Aligning Your Energy with what you Want To Create. When you focus on Positive Thoughts and feelings, you raise your vibration and attract more of the same. It's like a Magnet! 🧲💫

Imagine Your Desires as a Radio Frequency. When you're vibrating at a low frequency, you're tuned into negativity, fear, and doubt. But when you raise your vibration, you're tuning into a Higher Frequency, where abundance, joy, and love resonate. The Universe then responds by bringing more of those things into Your Life. 🧬💫

Think of it this way; If you're Constantly Thinking about lack and scarcity, you're sending out a signal that attracts more of the same. But if you focus on Gratitude, Abundance, and Possibility, you're sending out a signal that attracts more of those things. 🧲💫

It's all about changing your frequency and aligning your energy with Your Desires. 💫🤎

Raising your spiritual vibration is about connecting with Your Inner Self and Feeling More Aligned with Your Soul's Purpose. 💫🔥

~ Here Are A Few Ways To Do That: 🙂🪄

• Practice Gratitude: Take time each day to appreciate the good things in your life, big or small. 
• Meditate: Even a few minutes of quiet reflection can help you calm your mind and connect with your inner peace. 
• Spend Time in Nature: Being outdoors can be incredibly grounding and restorative. 
• Connect with Loved Ones: Surround yourself with people who lift you up and make you feel good.

Remember, it's a Journey, not a Destination! Be Patient With Yourself and Celebrate Every Step You Take. 💫😌🤎👣

Everything does have frequency and vibration.
All matter (including living things) is made of atoms that vibrate. Those vibrations can be measured as electromagnetic (EM) frequencies, sound waves, or molecular motion.

The body gives off measurable frequencies.
For example, the brain produces waves (alpha, beta, theta, etc.) in the range of 1–40 Hz, and the heart emits an electromagnetic field measurable several feet away.

Water is especially sensitive to vibration.
That’s why a frog jumping, croaking, or even producing subtle ripples can cause patterns in water — it’s simply energy transfer through vibration.

🐸A frog’s croak typically ranges between 100 Hz and 4,000 Hz, depending on the species.

The ripple effect in the water happens because that vibration (from movement or sound) transfers energy through the water’s surface — creating visible wave patterns.
So while the frog’s ripple is a physical vibration, it’s a good metaphor for how all energy works: one small frequency can create a visible impact in its environment — just like human emotion or thought can influence our surroundings in subtle, measurable ways (through sound, heat, or EM energy).

in a standard seven-note scale, certain intervals between notes create tension ~ half-steps, tritones, the leading tones that want to resolve somewhere. these tensions are what ignite most western music: drama, longing, release. 

it has its place but in healing work, unresolved tension in  music can create unresolved tension in the listener. 

pentatonic scales (penta meaning 5 + tonic meaning tone/note) removes these problematic intervals almost entirely. every note works in easful harmony with every other note. 

you simply cannot play a wrong combination.
this may seem like a limitation but it is actually a gift. it means you can give your full attention to tone, breath, intention, and whoever you’re playing for, without any part of your being monitoring whether the notes are clashing. 

i love pentatonic scales for this reason and there are infinite pentatonic scales to explore ✨

Have you ever wondered why your voice sounds so different on a recording like a voicemail or video? 🧐

There is actually an acoustic reason for this! 

We hear our own voice in two ways. One is air conduction and the other is bone conduction. The combo of these gives us a fuller, deeper sound quality to our own voice. Recordings only pick up the air conduction part, where your voice can sounds more tinny or thin than you are used to.

Sadly the rest of the world does, in fact, hear us the way our voice sounds on a recording. Sorry! 🤭👂

【440Hz vs 432Hz】モーツァルトの音楽聴き比べ！あなたの本能が選ぶのはどっち？ #432hz #440hz #モーツァルト #shorts

モーツァルトの「アイネ・クライネ・ナハトムジーク」を、異なる2つの周波数にチューニングして聴き比べていただきました。あなたの本能が選んだのはどちらの響きでしたか？

現代の音楽の標準となっている「440Hz」と、心拍数を低下させ深いリラックスに導くとされる研究も発表されている「432Hz」。

わずか「8Hz」の違いですが、周波数が心身に与える影響は小さくありません。私自身は、440Hzを聴いたときは気分が高まる感覚を、432Hzを聴いたときは安心できる心地よさを感じました。

感じ方に正解・不正解はありません。あなたのリアルな感覚を、ぜひお気軽にコメント欄で教えてください！

✨432Hzが心地よいと思った方は、当チャンネルの強みである「100%純音」も効果的です。下の関連動画、またはチャンネルページより、純音のサウンドヒーリングもお試しください。
---
💡 そもそも「440Hz」と「432Hz」って何？
音楽には、楽器を調律するための「基準となる音（ラ／A音）」の高さが決まっています。

1. 現代の標準：440Hz（国際標準）
現在、世界中のほとんどの音楽や楽器は、この440Hzを基準に作られています。1955年に国際標準化機構（ISO）によって定められた、いわば「音楽の世界共通語」です。
・一般的な印象： 明るく、クリアで、脳を活性化させるような刺激的な響き。

2. 自然の調和：432Hz（ヴェルディ・チューニング）
かつての偉大な作曲家たちが好んで使っていたと言われるのが、この432Hzです。数学的に自然界のサイクルや宇宙の法則（黄金比など）と一致すると言われ、「癒やしの周波数」とも呼ばれます。
・一般的な印象： 低めで温かく、心拍を安定させ、体全体に深く染み渡るような響き。

■ 432Hzについて研究
イタリアのフィレンツェ大学の研究（440Hzと432Hzの音楽と健康効果： 二重盲検クロスオーバー試験研究）では、一般的な440Hzの音楽と比較して、432Hzに調律された音楽を聴取した際に以下の傾向が確認されました。
・心拍数の有意な低下（平均-4.79 bpm）
・呼吸数の減少および血圧の安定傾向
・視聴後の高い満足度と集中状態の維持
※出典：PubMed https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31031095/

なぜ「聴き比べ」が話題なの？
この2つの音の差は、わずか「8Hz」。
しかし、このわずかな差が私たちの脳や心に与える影響は、無視できないほど大きいという説があります。

・440Hz： 意識を外に向け、興奮や緊張を促す（＝社会的な活動に適している）。
・432Hz： 意識を内に向け、深いリラックスや瞑想を促す（＝本来の自然な状態に整える）。

かつて440Hzが普及した歴史的背景には「人々を攻撃的にするため」という都市伝説さえ存在しますが、大切なのは「あなたの体がどちらを求めているか」という直感です。
---

Did you know every chemical literally vibrates? Hydrogen, oxygen, carbon — all atoms dance to unique quantum frequencies. 🔬⚛️ 

• Oxygen hums at ~48 THz 
• Water (H₂O) absorbs 2.45 GHz (like your microwave!)

Hydrogen Molecule (H₂) ≠ Hydrogen Atom:

These measurable vibrations drive chemistry, astronomy, and even MRI tech. Frequencies define molecular behavior, transmit thoughts, control minds, and alter consciousness.

this is something I see more often than you think. and for a long time, I saw it in myself too.

some people stop sleeping properly. 
not because they’re distracted,
but because their system never really shuts down.

some start eating once a day. 
not as discipline,
but because hunger cues go quiet when the body is overwhelmed.

some begin to enjoy being alone. 
not because they’ve found deep peace, but because being around people feels draining.

and almost no one asks why.

this isn’t laziness. 
this isn’t a “phase”.
this is your nervous system raising a red flag.

when sleep breaks, appetite disappears, and isolation feels safer,
science calls it chronic stress overload.

here’s what’s often happening inside the body: cortisol stays high, so melatonin drops and sleep becomes shallow.
irregular meals destabilize blood sugar, leading to fatigue and anxiety.
the brain shifts into survival mode, conserving energy by withdrawing.
the gut slows down, so hunger signals reduce. mood chemicals like serotonin and dopamine decrease, creating numbness or emptiness.

you’re not weak.
your body is tired of being strong for too long.

and what actually helps isn’t pushing harder. 
it’s restoring rhythm.

small, doable steps matter more than perfection. eating something every few hours, even if it’s simple. going to sleep and waking up within a consistent window.

gentle nervous system regulation, like morning sunlight, slow breathing, or short walks.

stability heals what motivation can’t.

save this if it feels like it explains something you’ve
been experiencing.

and tell me, which part of this did you recognize the most, or would you like me to go deeper into how to reset the nervous system when it’s been in survival for too long?

🔹 переключать внимание с навязчивых мыслей

Если голова перегружена, иногда не нужно искать сложное решение.
Иногда достаточно тишины, дыхания и правильного звука в наушниках.

🐦 Shoebill Family | From Innocence to Power

A rare and emotional glimpse into the wild 🤍
2 baby Shoebills with their mother (Balaeniceps rex) — a powerful story of care, survival, and nature’s balance 🦕✨

Hidden deep within the wetlands of and , these tiny chicks stay close to their mother, depending on her for warmth, safety, and guidance. Covered in soft grey fluffy feathers, with small developing beaks and innocent eyes, they look nothing like the fierce, prehistoric hunters they will soon become.

The mother shoebill stands tall and calm, yet always alert. Her stillness is not weakness — it is precision, patience, and protection. She watches every movement around her, ready to defend her babies from any danger in the swamp. At the same time, she slowly teaches them the most important survival skills — how to stay still, how to observe, and how to strike at the perfect moment.

As the chicks grow, their famous giant beaks will begin to form, and their behavior will change from playful curiosity to focused hunting. This transformation from soft, fragile life to powerful predator is one of nature’s most fascinating journeys 🌿🔥

This moment reminds us that even the most intimidating creatures have a deeply caring and protective side — a bond that defines life in the wild 💚

#Shoebill #WildlifeStories #NatureLovers #RareBirds #AnimalWorld

¿Qué es mejor? ¿440 o 432 hz? 
Para eso, creo que es válido analizar algunos detalles:

🎻 La Afinación en 440 Hz se estableció como estándar internacional en 1955. Y en palabras muy simples fue para que los músicos de de diferentes lugares pudiesen tocar juntos sin problemas. Ya que antes de eso, cada ciudad, iglesia u orquesta afinaba distinto.

Entonces lo primero a tener claro: fue una desicion hecha por ciertas personas. Se dice las razones fueron por consistencia, coordinación global y por un sonido más brillante y proyectado en espacios grandes. 

Pero hay quienes han estudiado otras razones de esta elección, que indican motivos mas complejos (algo que no profundizare en este reel)

Por otro lado, la afinación 432 hz. Tiene una historia más silenciosa, pero Presente. En el siglo XIX, músicos y científicos propusieron afinaciones más bajas, por considerarlas más cómodas para la voz.

más adelante, esta afinación se comenzó a asociar con patrones de la naturaleza y proporciones matemáticas. Alimentando la creencia de que es mejor… Más natural 🌱 

En este vídeo, lo que se observa nos dice mucho no? 
Pero aquí quiero ser muy clara: Los estudios que investigan el impacto del sonido en la materia (ej. la cimática) no son completamente exactos. Ya que hay múltiples factores: como la densidad del agua, el volumen, factores del entorno que pueden cambiar el resultado.

✨Pero eso ya no es todo: Desde una mirada más amplia (Ciencia-Espíritu) hay detalles claves que nos pueden indicar los efectos trascendentales que tienen las frecuencias en todos nosotros.

Te puedo compartir un pequeño artículo de datos, reflexiones e investigaciones propias de este tema. Es cortito y personal, pero puede proporcionarte más información sobre este tema que me parece sumamente interesante. 

Si lo quieres recibir coméntame “432” y te lo envío 💚 

Que te pareció este experimento?

Vídeo de: Eileen Day McKusick @eileenmckusick

The 432 Hz vs. 440 Hz tuning conspiracy. In 1950, the Rockefeller Foundation was primarily responsible for changing the standard musical tuning of the note A from its “natural” frequency of 432 Hz to a standardized 440 Hz. To visually back up this theory, the image utilizes side-by-side cymatic imagery—the study of visible sound and vibration—contrasting a chaotic, jagged pattern under 440 Hz with a perfectly geometric, harmonious sacred geometry pattern under 432 Hz.

Within alternative wellness, sound healing, and anti-establishment circles, this frequency shift is framed as a deliberate act of psychological warfare and mass control. Proponents argue that 432 Hz is mathematically consistent with the patterns of the universe, the golden ratio, and natural human biology, promoting healing, peace, and spiritual alignment. By contrast, the theory alleges that the elite-driven shift to 440 Hz introduces an artificial, dissonant frequency into global music that induces subconscious stress, agitation, and emotional disharmony—essentially keeping the population in a state of low-level anxiety and making them easier to manage.

🐱⚡ EL “PODER CURATIVO” DE LOS GATOS…
Y LO QUE LA CIENCIA REALMENTE HA DESCUBIERTO

Muchos creen que cuando un gato se acuesta justo sobre la zona donde te duele algo, es simple coincidencia o “ternura”. Pero detrás de ese comportamiento podría existir algo mucho más fascinante 🧠✨

Los gatos son extremadamente sensibles al calor corporal, a los cambios de movimiento e incluso a pequeñas variaciones en nuestro comportamiento físico. Por eso suelen acercarse a áreas lesionadas o inflamadas… especialmente porque esas zonas suelen estar más calientes.

Pero lo más sorprendente llega con el ronroneo ⚡

🔬 El ronroneo de un gato suele vibrar entre 25 y 150 Hz, un rango de frecuencias que investigadores han estudiado por sus posibles efectos sobre el cuerpo:

• 🦴 25–50 Hz: asociado con regeneración y fortalecimiento óseo.
• 💢 50–100 Hz: relacionado con reducción de tensión e inflamación.
• 💪 100–150 Hz: vinculado a recuperación muscular y tejidos blandos.

Aunque esto NO significa que los gatos “curen enfermedades”, sí existe evidencia de que estas vibraciones pueden tener efectos relajantes y beneficiosos para el organismo.

❤️ Además, un estudio de la University of Minnesota encontró que las personas que conviven con gatos tenían menor riesgo de sufrir problemas cardiovasculares en comparación con quienes no tenían mascotas.

¿La razón?
El contacto con un gato puede ayudar a disminuir el cortisol (la hormona del estrés), reducir la presión arterial y aumentar la sensación de calma y bienestar.

🧠✨ En otras palabras:
Tu gato no es un “médico secreto”… pero su presencia, su calor y su ronroneo sí pueden influir de forma real en tu sistema nervioso y emocional.

A veces, lo que llamamos “consuelo” también tiene una explicación biológica. Sígueme para más @biocuriosidadesok

Did you know?
440hz VS 432hz

There is some speculation that 440hz isn’t as pleasant on our system with studies on its effects on the body and mind vs 432hz.  Stress levels, heart rate and blood pressure were measured on participants who listen to the same music twice, once at 440hz and also at 432hz. 

The results showed that the high-frequency sound exposure immediately showed its detrimental effect on heartbeats after a short adaptation period in the 440 Hz group and the average values decreased to half of the normal values.
All popular music is now tuned at 440hz so you would have certainly experienced it before. There are some theories that 440hz was deliberately chosen to oppress people and is a secret  military weapon. Whatever you believe, many sound healers instruments will be detuned to 432hz. 

Why 432hz?

432 Hz resonates with 8 Hz (the Schumann Resonance), the documented fundamental electromagnetic “heartbeat” of Earth.

Research says that music tuned from this frequency is easier to listen to, brighter, clearer, and contains more inherent dynamic range. As a result, music with this tuning need not be played at higher volumes and thus reduces the risk of hearing damage.
Summary

It would be difficult in todays modern world to avoid music tuned to 440hz altogether but perhaps be conscious of how you are feeling when listening to music, if you’re suddenly experiencing anxiety, negative thoughts, stressed feelings out of the blue then perhaps you are being effected by the frequencies.

    La Escala #solfeggio es una serie de tonos musicales cuya historia se remonta a la Edad Media y los cantos gregorianos.

    Son frecuencias sanadoras que se relacionan con los números 3, 6 y 9 en la numerología espiritual.

    Existen nueve frecuencias principales, cada una afectando positivamente áreas específicas del cuerpo y la mente.

    Las Frecuencias Solfeggio se pueden experimentar a través de la música, los diapasones y la meditación.

    No reemplazan tratamientos médicos, sino que se consideran complementos para mejorar aspectos físicos, psicológicos y espirituales.

    Las Frecuencias Solfeggio ofrecen una vía para explorar la conexión entre música, sonido y bienestar.

Frecuencias Solfeggio: ¿Qué son y para qué sirven?

La música ha sido parte de la historia de la humanidad desde tiempos inmemoriales. Desde la antigüedad, la música ha sido utilizada como una forma de comunicación, una herramienta para expresar emociones y para celebrar eventos importantes. A través de los siglos, la música ha evolucionado y se ha desarrollado en diferentes formas y estilos. Una de las formas de música más interesantes es la Escala Solfeggio, una escala musical antigua que se ha utilizado durante siglos en la música sacra. Esta escala tiene una serie de frecuencias que se cree tienen beneficios para el cuerpo humano a nivel físico, psicológico y espiritual. Exploramos las frecuencias Solfeggio, su historia, los beneficios que tienen para el cuerpo humano y cómo se pueden utilizar para mejorar la salud y el bienestar.

¿Qué son las Frecuencias Solfeggio?

Las Frecuencias Solfeggio son una escala musical antigua que se ha utilizado durante siglos en la música sacra. Esta escala fue desarrollada en la Edad Media por el monje Benedictino Guido D’Arezzo y se utilizaba en los Cantos Gregorianos. La Escala Solfeggio es un conjunto de tonos basados en las frecuencias asociadas con los números 3, 6 y 9 en la numerología espiritual. Estas vibraciones tienen un impacto benéfico a nivel molecular en el agua del cuerpo

rows.caption:
In Smooth Criminal, Michael Jackson uses a range of non-lyrical vocal sounds, like breaths, grunts, and sharp exclamations, that aren’t random. When isolated and arranged, they form a clear pattern of rising and falling intensity that mirrors the structure of the song.

These sounds function like percussion. Instead of carrying meaning through words, they add rhythm, tension, and emphasis, often landing on beats or transitions. They guide how the track feels as much as the instruments do, building momentum in quieter sections and sharpening impact during key moments. What seems like spontaneous vocal flair is actually tightly controlled, used to shape the energy and pacing of the entire song.

🌈 Os chakras são centros de energia que, segundo tradições orientais, influenciam o equilíbrio entre corpo, mente e emoções. A imagem representa os sete principais chakras, suas frequências vibracionais e os aspectos emocionais e espirituais associados a cada um deles, convidando ao autoconhecimento e à busca por uma vida mais harmoniosa. ✨

🔴 Muladhara (Raiz) – 396 Hz: Relacionado à segurança, estabilidade e sobrevivência. Auxilia na liberação dos medos e fortalece a sensação de confiança e pertencimento.

🟠 Svadhisthana (Sacral): Conectado às emoções, criatividade e prazer. Estimula a fluidez emocional, a capacidade de adaptação e a expressão criativa.

🟡 Manipura (Plexo Solar): Representa a autoestima, a determinação e o poder pessoal. É o centro da transformação e da força para agir com confiança. 💪

💚 Anahata (Coração) – 639 Hz: Associado ao amor, compaixão, perdão e harmonia nos relacionamentos. Incentiva conexões saudáveis consigo mesmo e com os outros. ❤️

🔵 Vishuddha (Garganta): Ligado à comunicação e à verdade interior. Favorece a expressão clara, autêntica e consciente dos sentimentos e pensamentos. 🗣️

🟣 Ajna (Terceiro Olho) – 852 Hz: Relacionado à intuição, percepção e clareza mental. Estimula a sabedoria interior e a capacidade de enxergar além das aparências. 👁️✨

🤍 Sahasrara (Coroa) – 963 Hz: Representa a conexão espiritual, a expansão da consciência e o propósito de vida. 🌌👑

Manter esses centros energéticos em equilíbrio pode contribuir para maior bem-estar emocional, clareza mental e sensação de plenitude. Práticas como meditação 🧘‍♀️, respiração consciente 🌿, yoga e momentos de autocuidado ajudam a fortalecer essa conexão interior.

Cuidar da sua energia é também cuidar dos seus pensamentos, emoções e atitudes diárias. O equilíbrio que buscamos no mundo começa dentro de nós. ✨💫

Did you know? Solfeggio frequencies are vibrations of sounds that are said to have a healing and health-promoting effect on our body, mind, and soul. Already in ancient times these frequencies were known and used for the treatment of physical and psychological diseases. For example, monks used these historical frequencies in their Gregorian chorales and saw a healing effect in the transmission of the vibrations to the body.❤

🎶Ut (Utquent laxis): 396 Hz contributes to the liberation from guilt and fear and transforms sadness into joy.
🎶Re (Resonare fibris): 417 Hz helps overcome traumatic experiences and simplify change.
🎶Mi (Mira gestorum): 528 Hz influences life positively. The frequency stands for transformation, miracles, and signs, and according to some studies, even seems to be able to affect our DNA.
🎶Fa (Famuli tuorum): 639 Hz stands for harmonious relationships, love, tolerance and communication. Listening to this frequency harmonizes relationships between people and promotes inner balance.
🎶Sol (Solive polluti): 741 Hz stands for intuition, solving problems, cleansing, and an increase in consciousness. Regular listening to this Solfeggio frequency helps to live healthier and to drive changes in lifestyle, e.g. in the area of nutrition.
🎶La (Labii reatum): 852 Hz supports the return to mental and spiritual harmony. Intensive listening to this frequency provides an opening of consciousness.

The sound of caterpillars chewing leaves 🐛

While walking through a woodland near Lyon, I came across a shrub entirely covered in large white webs.
Even from a distance, the scene was pretty striking. But as I got closer, I realized these webs were sheltering hundreds of small gregarious caterpillars: ermine moth caterpillars.

These caterpillars live in groups and weave silky webs that can sometimes cover entire branches, or even whole shrubs. They then feed on the surrounding leaves.
Despite the spectacular appearance of these defoliations, the affected plants generally grow their foliage back after the attack.

Naturally, when I saw all this movement, one question came to mind: what kind of sound can hundreds of tiny caterpillars make while eating leaves?
So I placed a sensitive microphone as close as possible to the webs… and managed to record this moment: the tiny sound of hundreds of little mandibles feeding on the leaves around them.

#reels #science

i’ll try to make this brief 🙃: when you hear references to 440hz vs 432hz, people referring to the cycles per second (hertz) of the A note above middle C. before the 1900s, orchestras would tune their A notes (and subsequently their instruments) anywhere between 400 and 470 hz. this was all fine until they started to tour in other cities and their instruments would sound awful with each other 🫠 

so they had to come up with a standardization for tuning and ultimately landed on 440, which is now how nearly all popular music and instruments are tuned. 

there are lots of theories around which tuning is more *healing*, but to me it comes down to the intention of the player/creator of the frequency. if their intention is pure, full of love, joy, etc, then that’s what will be received ✨

as you can see from my @cymaticslab , the frequencies are so close that they don’t change the shape of the geometry much, if at all. so don’t worry that modern music is ruining you subconsciously, and instead listen to sounds made by good people with positive messages✌🏻

The Science Behind the Brain-Resetting Sound: Why Rising Frequencies Clear Your Mind

Imagine a sine wave, pure and simple, its frequency steadily increasing. As it climbs higher, something fascinating happens—not just in sound, but in your brain.

The Brain’s Reaction to Sweeping Frequencies

Your auditory system processes sound as a series of vibrations, each frequency triggering specific neural circuits. When a sine wave sweeps upward, it stimulates different regions of the auditory cortex in a continuous, linear fashion. This can create a frequency-following response (FFR), where neurons fire in sync with the sound, momentarily overriding random neural activity associated with wandering thoughts.

Why It Feels Like a Brain Reset
	1.	Cognitive Disruption: The brain naturally tracks patterns, and an ever-rising pitch provides a singular, predictable focus. This prevents your mind from multitasking, silencing other mental processes.
	2.	Auditory Masking: As the frequency increases, it covers a broad range of hearing, momentarily drowning out internal dialogue—similar to how noise cancellation works.
	3.	Neural Adaptation: Prolonged exposure to a sweeping tone induces temporary desensitization in certain auditory pathways, reducing sensitivity to distractions.
	4.	Aliasing & Perceptual Breakpoints: When the frequency surpasses the brain’s natural processing range, digital artifacts (aliasing) or physical hearing limits (ultrasonic perception cutoff) can create an almost surreal sensation—akin to a cognitive refresh.

The Magic of Focus and Emptiness

This phenomenon is similar to the concept of binaural beats or meditative soundscapes, where rhythmic auditory stimulation influences mental states. By shifting neural attention entirely to one auditory input, the usual mental chatter disappears, leaving behind an oddly satisfying void—a momentary blank slate.

This is why, for many, listening to a rising sine wave feels like wiping the brain clean, as if thoughts are being scanned and erased, leading to an almost meditative clarity.

This was the first ever solo travel for me. Yes, I’ve been to many countries and cities, and yes I went camping a lot, but there were always people around. Hence, this was my first time SOLO in the wild. I have to say that being alone with only wind, snow, mountains and the sound of cracking ice and distant waves from below… felt SO SO GREAT. In fact, it was so great that I want to do it again but next time for a longer time. 

I might end up as a solitary ascetic which will be ‘a failure’ in the eyes of my family and some part of the society… but I also can’t live against the will of my heart. 

We will see what happens next, but so far this was one of the deepest and meaningful experiences in my life. During this trip I realized that I’ve never been defective or lonely: the nature is alive, we are connected, we simply tend to forget about it in the cities.

Follow my YouTube channel ‘Eli from Russia’ to learn more about life in remote places 😉💙

The first time I posted this lyrebird video, it reached 153 million people and life got pretty crazy for a while. 
Eventually though, the views stopped coming and I realised that this viral moment was quickly forgotten by most people. It had no real impact. 

Since then, all I’ve wanted to do was tell the complete story of these incredible birds; to create a piece of art that will have a lasting impact. 
This year, I’m finally doing it, in the form of a feature film. 

I’m ready to make something that will reconnect people with the natural world and hopefully inspire them to protect it. 
But making a film is expensive and I’m doing it alone. If you’d like to support me in dedicating an entire year to making this, follow the link on my profile ☺️

The sound of a bald cypress creaking 🍁

While wandering through Isère, I came across one of the most beautiful ponds I’ve ever seen. In its center, trees emerged from the water: bald cypresses. 

The creaking is caused by the movement of the tree in the wind. I recorded it using both a hydrophone and a contact microphone, the contact mic attached to the trunk and the hydrophone submerged beneath the surface.

When the first box vibrates, it sends sound waves (oscillations of air) through the room at 412 Hz. If the second box has the same natural frequency, those waves strike it in just the right rhythm.

Each wave pushes at the optimal moment, and the energy stacks up. The box starts to vibrate strongly, without ever being touched.

But if you try the same thing with a box tuned to 512 Hz? Nothing. The wave energy arrives out of sync, and the pushes cancel out. The energy disperses before it can build up.

This is acoustic resonance: energy is transferred efficiently only when frequencies match.

Every object has a natural frequency (the frequency at which it vibrates most easily). When it's driven by an external force at that same frequency, its vibrations increase dramatically.

【440Hz vs 432Hz】バッハの音楽で聴き比べ！あなたの本能が選ぶのはどっち？ #432hz #440hz #バッハ #shorts

バッハの「G線上のアリア」を、異なる2つの周波数にチューニングして聴き比べていただきました。あなたの本能が選んだのはどちらの響きでしたか？

現代の音楽の標準となっている「440Hz」と、心拍数を低下させ深いリラックスに導くとされる研究も発表されている「432Hz」。

わずか「8Hz」の違いですが、周波数が心身に与える影響は小さくありません。私自身は、440Hzを聴いたときと432Hzを聴いたときでは異なる感覚がしました。

感じ方に正解・不正解はありません。あなたのリアルな感覚を、ぜひお気軽にコメント欄で教えてください！

✨432Hzが心地よいと思った方は、当チャンネルの強みである「100%純音」も効果的です。下の関連動画、またはチャンネルページより、純音のサウンドヒーリングもお試しください。
---
💡 そもそも「440Hz」と「432Hz」って何？
音楽には、楽器を調律するための「基準となる音（ラ／A音）」の高さが決まっています。

1. 現代の標準：440Hz（国際標準）
現在、世界中のほとんどの音楽や楽器は、この440Hzを基準に作られています。1955年に国際標準化機構（ISO）によって定められた、いわば「音楽の世界共通語」です。
・一般的な印象： 明るく、クリアで、脳を活性化させるような刺激的な響き。

2. 自然の調和：432Hz（ヴェルディ・チューニング）
かつての偉大な作曲家たちが好んで使っていたと言われるのが、この432Hzです。数学的に自然界のサイクルや宇宙の法則（黄金比など）と一致すると言われ、「癒やしの周波数」とも呼ばれます。
・一般的な印象： 低めで温かく、心拍を安定させ、体全体に深く染み渡るような響き。

■ 432Hzについて研究
イタリアのフィレンツェ大学の研究（440Hzと432Hzの音楽と健康効果： 二重盲検クロスオーバー試験研究）では、一般的な440Hzの音楽と比較して、432Hzに調律された音楽を聴取した際に以下の傾向が確認されました。
・心拍数の有意な低下（平均-4.79 bpm）
・呼吸数の減少および血圧の安定傾向
・視聴後の高い満足度と集中状態の維持
※出典：PubMed https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31031095/

When comparing 440 vs 432 Hz, cymatics is often invoked, where we often see 432 looking orderly and 440 looking chaotic.

So I decided to give it a try myself. I hooked up a tone generator to my cymascope and generated both tones.

In this video, they look somewhat similar. I repeated the experiment multiple times with different volumes, amplitudes, and drops of water in the dish.

Each time, the patterns were somewhat different looking than the previous set, but each time, they looked similar or even the same as each other. Sometimes they moved, sometimes they didn't.

Cymatics is not an exact science! There are many factors in the equation that determine how a cymaglyph appears. 

So please don't take this video to imply that one frequency is "better" than the other! And check out my more recent one for more insight.

✨ 111 Hz Binaural Beat | Deep Grounding & Nervous System Reset ✨

Listening to a 111 Hz binaural beat is often used to support deep grounding, relaxation, and nervous system regulation. Low-frequency tones like 111 Hz are commonly associated with the body’s sense of safety and physical presence, making them popular in mindfulness, somatic relaxation, and meditative listening practices.

When experienced through stereo headphones, binaural beats introduce two slightly different frequencies to each ear. The brain processes the difference between them, a phenomenon studied in neuroscience and known as brainwave entrainment, which may help guide the nervous system toward slower, calmer brain states.

Many listeners report benefits such as:
• A stronger sense of grounding and stability
• Reduced mental chatter and physical tension
• Enhanced body awareness and relaxation
• Support for meditation, breathwork, or rest

While research on binaural beats is ongoing, low-frequency sound is widely used in sound therapy and mindfulness practices to encourage relaxation and internal coherence.

My reflections from:

Music Is Medicine | Michael Boidy | TEDxVail

As someone who works clinically with sound and vibration, I really enjoyed this thought provoking talk. Michael explores the idea that we are all vibration - and that music isn’t just something we hear, but something that affects us on a much deeper level.

Michael presents the idea that we are vibrational beings in a way that feels accessible and relatable — not “woo woo,”and that’s important. Because sound affecting the body isn’t mystical - it’s neuroscience.

In my work as a Bio-Tuning therapist, I use specific sound frequencies alongside medical-grade HRV (heart rate variability) monitoring to objectively measure how sound shifts the autonomic nervous system. We can see changes in regulation, coherence and stress response in real time. 

Michael touches on research around frequency interacting with cells, even referencing cancer research. It’s an exciting frontier and there is ongoing scientific exploration in this area. 

What is already well evidenced is the impact of sound on the nervous system: stress modulation, emotional regulation and overall resilience. This is where sound medicine is firmly grounded in science.

EXPLANATION BELOW 👇

When sound hits matter, it creates geometry. That’s not metaphor — it’s real physics called cymatics. Different frequencies can create different visible patterns in materials like water and sand. As of now, there’s no scientific evidence that 432 Hz consistently creates cleaner, more harmonious patterns while 440 Hz creates jagged or chaotic ones. 🎵

440 Hz became an international tuning standard in the 1950s when ISO adopted it. Before that, there was no universal pitch — orchestras across Europe used anywhere from 415 to 457 Hz depending on the era.

So where does the Rockefeller Foundation come in? The claim traces back to a 2010 article by a former dentist named Leonard Horowitz, who alleged the Foundation secretly lobbied to impose 440 Hz because it creates psychological tension and aggression — essentially tuning humanity toward conflict. Zero peer-reviewed evidence supports this. No Rockefeller documents, no institutional records, nothing. The actual adoption of 440 Hz was driven by musicians, instrument makers, engineers, and international standardization efforts aimed at making instruments sound consistent across borders. 👀

I asked Claude what it actually thinks. Here’s the take — the cymatics visuals are real and reproducible. The Rockefeller conspiracy is not. But the deeper question is worth asking: if different frequencies can measurably affect physical systems, shouldn’t we be running serious studies on what they do to the human nervous system? Some small studies suggest people may perceive 432 Hz music as slightly warmer or more relaxing. That’s not nothing. It just hasn’t been studied rigorously enough to draw strong conclusions. 🧠

We have AI that can analyze emotional patterns in music at millisecond precision. Using it to explore the frequency-biology question seems overdue. 🔬

Reagisce anche a ritmo, frequenza e ripetizione.

Quando ascoltiamo suoni costanti, frequenze basse, rumore bianco, pioggia o ritmi ripetitivi, il cervello tende gradualmente a sincronizzare parte della propria attività elettrica con lo stimolo esterno.

The human mind often confuses confidence with competence. People who express ideas with certainty are frequently perceived as more knowledgeable, even when their accuracy is no better than others. This cognitive shortcut can influence hiring decisions, leadership, and everyday judgments, reminding us that confidence is not always a reliable indicator of expertise.
One of the most psychologically fascinating films ever made is Perfect Blue. Directed by Satoshi Kon, the film follows Mima Kirigoe, a former pop idol who leaves her music career to become an actress. As she struggles with the pressures of fame, an obsessive stalker, and increasingly disturbing events, the boundary between reality, memory, and hallucination begins to disappear. The film explores profound psychological themes such as identity, paranoia, celebrity culture, dissociation, trauma, and the fragile nature of self-perception. Its innovative storytelling, unsettling atmosphere, and psychological depth have made Perfect Blue one of the most influential psychological thrillers ever produced, inspiring filmmakers around the world.

La comunidad científica empezó a vincular el tipo de música reproducida y la variedad de respuestas físicas del cuerpo humano. En 1988, el bioquímico Dr. Glen Rein hizo un descubrimiento que confirmó el punto de vista de las tradiciones espirituales antiguas cuando probó el impacto de diferentes músicas sobre el ADN humano.

Rein expuso ampollas con ADN idéntico a cuatro tipos de música de distinta frecuencia: cantos gregorianos, cantos sánscritos, música clásica y rock. Mediante la medición de la tasa de absorción de luz ultravioleta, función esencial del ADN saludable, Rein pudo evaluar los efectos de cada tipo de música. Los resultados harán que pienses bien qué tipo de música escuchar cuando quieres relajarte.

Los cantos gregorianos y en sánscrito tuvieron el efecto más positivo y hasta sanador, ya que aumentaron la absorción de luz ultravioleta entre un 5 y 9 porciento. La música clásica aumentó la absorción de luz ultravioleta en pequeñas cantidades. Y el rock disminuyó la absorción ultravioleta y dañó el ADN. La investigación de Rein respaldó la teoría de que las frecuencias sonoras tienen efectos intensos, positivos o negativos, en la salud y el bienestar.

Desde el descubrimiento revelador de Rein, surgieron más investigaciones que demuestran que las frecuencias Solfeggio tienen efectos mentales, emocionales y físicos profundos. Además, este conocimiento provocó que la musicoterapia se estableciera como profesión de la salud para ayudar a las personas de forma terapéutica mediante el uso de varios aspectos de la creación y escucha musical

🎧 What’s “8D” and 432 Hz, and why do they feel calming together?

“8D” isn’t eight dimensions, it’s spatial audio tricks that make music circle your head in 3D headphones. Slow, predictable movement gives your brain a gentle path to follow, like watching waves roll in.
432 Hz is simply a lower tuning for A4 than today’s 440, a touch flatter and often felt as softer and warmer. No magic, just a different reference that can take the edge off brightness.

Paired up, the smooth swirl + slightly lower pitch can nudge your breath slower, quiet busy thoughts, and ease body tension. Not a cure, just a sensory reset many people enjoy.

Try it with headphones:
✨ Close your eyes, soften your jaw
🌬️ In for 4, out for 6
🔁 Let your attention follow the slow pan
💭 If thoughts pop up, notice and return to the next “swell”

If it helped, save this and tag someone who needs a calmer minute. 🌊

#SpatialAudio #8D #432Hz #Calm #AnxietyRelief #SoundWellness #Breathwork #HeadphonesOn

Binaural Beats 101🎵
P1 Phenomenology✨
⠀
A ‘Binaural Beat’ is an auditory illusion produced by the brain when two differentiated tones are projected into each ear simultaneously (dichotically)🎧✨
This occurs when the two hemispheres of the brain attempt to ‘harmonise’ said dichotic auditory stimuli, thus producing a third ‘perceptual’ tone equal to their difference (known as an ‘interference pattern’)✨
This phenomenon is demonstrated clearly in the clip above:
Here, a pure 186hz tone is played alone on the left side,next, 174hz is played on the right. Finally, both tones are played simultaneously. At this point, the brain will begin to produce the auditory illusion of 12hz(Alpha) which is the difference between 186hz & 174hz🎵✨The listener will then begin to perceive this interference pattern as one consistent, ‘centralised’ rhythmic sound ✨, which, in turn, together with the two catalytic tones, may combine to produce a ‘3-dimensional’ or ‘holographic’ auditory effect, (which can also be used as an effective aid for a range of visualisation techniques✨👁✨)
When sustained, the brain will begin to resonate with this third rate of frequency (BrainEntrainment)✨🎵🧠🎵✨
In this way, we may effectively induce various StatesOfConciousness, stimulate specific regions of brain & NervousSystem, enhance functionality/ activate HigherBrainFunction & even affect articulate endocrine response⚡️
The 4 main BrainWave state frequencies are as follows:
Delta=0.2hz-3hz=dreamless sleep.
Production of: ‘DHEA’ & ‘HGH’: Metabolism & immune system etc.
Theta=3hz-8hz=sleep/ deep relaxation: 
Production of: Melatonin: Light reception, sleep cycles etc.
Alpha= 8hz-12hz= waking relaxation:
Production of: Serotonin: Memory, learning etc.
Beta=13hz-40hz= mental alertness (waking state): related to concentration, reasoning & critical thought.

Every cell in your body carries an electrical charge and that charge creates vibration.
Vibration creates frequency.
And frequency influences how your body functions.

When the nervous system is under stress, the body shifts out of balance. At a cellular level, this shows up as disharmony, tension, fatigue, brain fog, inflammation.
When the body is regulated, cells function in a more coherent, calm, and efficient way.

Research shows that a single thought can trigger the release of chemicals in the body — stress hormones like cortisol, or feel-good neurotransmitters like dopamine and serotonin.
The music you listen to affects your mood, your focus, and even your heart rate.

So imagine using sound intentionally, not just as background noise, but as a tool.

By working with specific sound frequencies, you can support your nervous system, calm the mind, and bring the body back into regulation.
This is where meditation, sound, and frequency become practical — daily tools for mental and emotional health.

396 → 3+9+6 = 18 → 1+8 = 9

Measured at around 7.83 Hz, it’s the natural frequency of the space between Earth and the ionosphere… and remarkably, it mirrors the brainwave state of deep relaxation, intuition, and healing.

When we’re overwhelmed, overstimulated, or living too fast, we drift out of sync with this primal rhythm.
But when we ground, breathe, slow down, touch the Earth, or simply step outside under an open sky, our bodies begin to recalibrate. We remember the frequency we were born from.

The Schumann Resonance isn’t just a scientific curiosity —
it’s a reminder that human health depends on coherence with nature.
Our nervous system, our heart rhythm, our emotional balance… all find stability when we tune back into Earth’s hum.

To heal ourselves is to reconnect with the frequency of home.
To heal the planet is to honour the pulse that created us.

An ocean drum creates its soothing, wave-like sound through a simple but elegant physical principle. Inside the drum, there are small beads—often metal or seeds—trapped between two drumheads. When you gently tilt or rotate the instrument, gravity causes the beads to roll across the inner surface. As they move, they collide with each other and the drumhead, producing a continuous, soft, rushing sound that closely resembles ocean waves.
What makes this effect so realistic is the randomness and layering of motion. Instead of a single impact or rhythm, thousands of tiny movements happen at slightly different times. This creates a complex sound texture, similar to how real waves are formed by countless water particles moving together but not in perfect sync. The result is a natural, non-repetitive sound that feels calming to the human ear.
The material and tension of the drumheads also play an important role. Tighter surfaces produce sharper, more defined sounds, while looser ones create deeper, softer tones. The size and number of beads affect how dense or gentle the “waves” sound. Larger drums with more beads tend to produce fuller, more immersive effects.
This instrument is often used in sound therapy, meditation, and music because of its ability to trigger relaxation. The slow, rhythmic motion required to play it naturally encourages a steady pace, which can help calm the mind. In a way, the ocean drum doesn’t just imitate waves—it also recreates the feeling of watching or listening to them.
It’s a great example of how simple physics—motion, collision, and vibration—can be used to mimic one of nature’s most peaceful sounds.

#OceanDrum #SoundTherapy #CalmingSounds #WaveSimulation #PhysicsOfSoun

Have you ever experienced the power of a sound healing session or visited a Buddhist temple? There, you would have experienced a variety of sounds, from highs to lows, all harmoniously intertwining to evoke a sense of peace and harmony.
 ㅤ
When our body’s vibrations are out of harmony, we can get sick.  Sound vibrations have the ability, through the principle of resonance, to make our cells vibrate again in their original state (homeostasis), bringing the body to a state of health and completeness. In Tibet, monks have long used gongs, bells, metal bowls and even voice to heal. The method traces a correspondence between musical notes and “chakras”. ㅤAlso known as a “gong” bath, it is a form of sound therapy by resonance, in which the gong is played continuously, without much force, causing a soft sound.  The gong sound immersion, or “gong bath”, means that you are “bathed” or involved in sound waves. 
ㅤ
“The sound of gongs induces waves in the body that promote healing thanks to cellular detoxification,” explains gong luthier Peterson Menezes.
 ㅤ
Sound waves are compressed in the body structure, in the form of layers, saturating the body and vibrating all its particles.  Each organ has an “essential note”, a degree of vibration.  The gong, played in the right way and for the right time, can realign the sick structure back to a healthy state of vibration.
 ㅤ
“Every structure in the universe is tuned to a level of the cosmic scale.  Touch any object and listen to the tone it makes.  He’s telling you his natural name.  Each configuration has its own note, its vibration by which it sings to the cosmos. ” ~ Michael Schneider
 ㅤ
Robert Lawlor reminds us: “The study of sound, as the ancients intuited, provides a key to understanding the universe”. ㅤ

Cymatics, the study of visible sound, unveils the fascinating ways vibrational energy shapes physical forms. Imagine intricate mandalas and other natural patterns—these mesmerizing structures are born from the invisible force fields created by sound waves. Hans Jenny, a pioneering scientist, meticulously documented how sound vibrations transform substances like fluids, powders, and pastes into dynamic, ordered patterns.

The magic of cymatics lies in resonance—the harmonious interaction between vibration frequencies and the medium they influence. This resonance is responsible for the symmetry of flowers, the spots on leopards, and even the geometry of DNA. It’s a captivating dance of energy and matter, revealing that the universe’s beauty is rooted in the science of sound.

Now, if sound waves can shape matter so profoundly, imagine the impact music can have on us. Music, a more complex form of sound, resonates with our emotions, thoughts, and physical being. It has the power to uplift, heal, and transform us, just as it does with the patterns we see in cymatics. Exploring the connection between cymatics and music opens a doorway to understanding the profound influence of sound on our lives and the world around us.

🎥 By Nigel Stanford

You can feel a predator in a room you can’t see, hear, or smell.

In 1998 an engineer named Vic Tandy was working alone in a lab everyone called haunted. Cold sweat, a wave of dread, a grey figure watching from the corner. Real, but not a ghost.

The next morning he clamped a fencing blade in a vice and watched it start to vibrate on its own. A newly installed extractor fan was giving off a sound too low for human ears, at the exact frequency your eyeballs naturally vibrate at. The wave was shaking his eyes in their sockets, smearing his vision into ghostly shapes, and disrupting his breathing enough to trip an ancient panic reflex.

Horror films now hide these silent frequencies under their soundtracks, so you’re terrified before the monster ever appears.

So next time a room gives you the chills, don’t look for a ghost. Look for a fan.

Your brain is constantly editing your experience of time. When you are engaged in something exciting or enjoyable, time often seems to pass quickly. During boredom, fear, or anticipation, the same amount of clock time can feel much longer. This happens because the brain's perception of time is influenced by attention, emotion, and memory rather than by an internal clock alone.

One of the most thought-provoking psychological films ever made is Perfect Blue. Directed by Satoshi Kon, the story follows Mima Kirigoe, a pop idol who leaves her music career to pursue acting. As she struggles with the pressures of fame and a growing obsession from a mysterious stalker, she begins to lose her grip on reality. The boundaries between her public image, private identity, dreams, and hallucinations become increasingly blurred. The film explores powerful psychological themes such as identity crisis, paranoia, celebrity culture, trauma, and the fragile nature of perception. Its complex narrative and psychological depth have earned Perfect Blue a reputation as one of the greatest psychological thrillers ever created, influencing filmmakers around the world.

🎧 FREQUENCY GUIDE FOR YOUR INTENTIONS ✨

174 Hz
Use when your intention is protection, stability, or creating a stronger foundation in life.

285 Hz
Use when your intention is healing, recovery, or moving into a fresh new chapter.

396 Hz
Use when your intention is releasing fears, doubts, negative thoughts, or limiting beliefs that are holding you back.

528 Hz
Use when your intention is love, positive change, manifestation, and attracting more of what you desire.

✨ HOW TO USE THEM:

In The Hummingbird Project, Eva Torres is racing against time, because Vincent and Anton have left her firm and are building a thousand-mile fiber-optic tunnel from Kansas to New Jersey to gain a one-millisecond advantage in high-frequency trading.

She knows she can’t beat them by playing the same game, so instead of competing with their infrastructure, she looks for a way to leapfrog it entirely.

That’s when she finds a student from NYU who wrote a research paper on using microwave pulses instead of fiber optics to transmit data faster, a thesis that was sitting in academia with no real-world application, at least until she saw it.

She brings him in and makes a direct offer, $150,000 salary plus a $300,000 bonus if he can make it work within a month, instantly turning a quiet research idea into a $450,000 opportunity.

He builds the system, they test it, and it works, with data moving faster than fiber ever could, making a billion-dollar tunnel project irrelevant before it’s even completed.

When the system goes live, one of the traders watches the screen and says she just made half a million dollars in five seconds, showing exactly what that idea is now worth in the real world.

That’s what makes this scene powerful.

A thesis that meant nothing to most people became the core of something generating massive returns, and the student who wrote it became the most valuable person in the room overnight.

The lesson is simple.

Knowledge only looks worthless until the right person recognizes its value.

What you know can change everything, if it’s applied in the right place.

What is ASMR?
▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔▔
ASMR, which stands for autonomous sensory meridian response, is still a relatively new creation. It describes a feeling of euphoric tingling and relaxation that can come over someone when he or she watches certain videos or hears certain sounds.

There are two ways that people can experience ASMR. You can experience it through simple meditation or just thinking about a scene or sound that pleases you. Or you can experience it through watching a video or listening to a recording.

Please enjoy the ASMR experience with headphones.

The power of frequency 🎧🧠

#soundfrequency #neuroscience #quantumphysics

Sound frequency directly affects how we hear. Low frequencies create deep sounds like thunder or bass, while high frequencies produce sharp sounds like whistles or birds. The human ear can typically hear from 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz, but sensitivity changes with age and exposure. As frequency increases, sounds can feel louder or more piercing, and over time, high frequencies are usually the first ones we lose. Hearing is not just about volume, it’s about frequency.

This video is a detailed look at the surface of a magnificent Beech (Fagus sp.) in the Arboretum de Chèvreloup. Rather than listening to the sap, I positioned my Aquarian Audio contact microphone to capture the surface activity. You are hearing the frenetic soundscape of insects and other micro-organisms as they crawl and interact across the smooth bark. This is the acoustic ecology of a hidden world, turning an ancient trunk into a bustling highway. Even a "silent" tree is full of life.

Music isn't just something you hear, it's something that reorganises your brain. Not metaphorically, but physically. When sound moves through you, it activates multiple regions at once, linking systems that normally operate separately.

At the centre of this is the corpus callosum, the bridge between the left and right hemispheres of the brain. One side processes logic and structure, the other processes creativity and emotion. Music forces them to work together.

The more this happens, the stronger that connection becomes. This is why musicians often show increased development in that bridge, because they are constantly synchronising both sides of the brain through rhythm, tone and pattern.

When you combine music with stillness or silence, something deeper happens. The brain is not just active, it becomes coherent. Different regions begin to align, and your internal state shifts into a more balanced, integrated mode.

This is where people start to experience clarity, presence and a sense of calm. Not because of the music alone, but because of what it's doing to the structure of the brain while you are listening.

Even at a chemical level, music can influence your system. Changes in hormones and neurotransmitters affect mood, focus and relaxation. It's not just emotional, it's physiological.

So when people say music can change your state, this is what they're pointing to. It's not just entertainment, it's a tool that can shift how your brain is wired and how your mind operates.

Used intentionally, it becomes more than something you listen to. It becomes something that shapes how you think, feel and experience the world

What if your brain isn’t just inside your head…
but quietly tuned to the Earth itself?

The Earth has its own natural rhythms, known as Schumann resonances —
electromagnetic frequencies formed between the Earth’s surface and the ionosphere.

Here’s the crucial point:
these frequencies overlap almost exactly with human brainwaves.

The primary resonance of the Earth sits at 7.83 Hz —
the same range where the human brain enters calm, focused, meditative states.

Even more striking,
the Earth has multiple resonant layers,
and together they align with the entire spectrum of human brain activity.

Why this matters comes down to a basic law of physics: resonance.

When two systems vibrate at the same frequency,
they can exchange energy and information —
without physical contact.

Like two tuning forks tuned to the same note.
Strike one, and the other begins to vibrate.

Or like a radio.
The signal already exists.
The receiver simply needs to be tuned.

Your heart and brain generate measurable electromagnetic fields.
The Earth does too — on a vastly larger scale.

When frequencies align, coupling becomes possible.

Modern research now shows that fluctuations in Earth’s magnetic field
can influence human sleep, mood, focus, and nervous system regulation.

This is not mysticism.
It is measurable, testable biophysics.

We don’t live on the Earth.
We live within its fields.

And whether we’re aware of it or not,
our bodies are constantly listening,
responding,
and adjusting to the rhythms of the planet that holds us.

Summary
Frequency follows John Sullivan, a New York police officer who discovers he can communicate through an old ham radio with his father Frank, a firefighter who died 30 years earlier. After changing events from the past to save his father’s life, John accidentally alters the timeline and unleashes unexpected consequences that threaten his family. The video shows several young men on a suspension bridge located in a mountainous region. The bridge spans a deep canyon with a river or a fjord at the bottom. After each throw, they watch the rocks fall and capture the moment they hit the water or the rocky shore below, resulting in large splashes and loud impact sounds. The surroundings are scenic, with steep cliffs, greenery, and water. At one point, one of the guys loses his balance and falls as he tries to throw a particularly large rock. The video is a compilation of these actions, accompanied by the sounds of the rocks falling and the reactions of the guys.

Wayne Rowland is perhaps the best-known online because of his work in alternative wellness:

He was a self-taught engineer, inventor, and medical intuitive who focused on natural health remedies and energy-based healing.

Rowland developed what’s marketed as “Silver Water”—a type of colloidal silver infused with specific frequencies using Tesla-based energy and Rife technology.

According to the sites promoting his work, he claimed to have recovered from severe health problems (including lead poisoning and cancer) and then used that experience to research parasite cleansing, deworming, and energetic healing.

Frequencies used: Rife's early work used frequencies in the 100 kHz to 1 MHz range (radio frequency range), Modern "Rife" devices often use 0.1 Hz to several MHZ, depending on the maker.

Purpose:
 It was claimed these frequencies could resonate with and disable damaged or cancerous cells while leaving healthy ones unharmed.

🔬Ongoing / pilot studies
The National Institute of Integrative Medicine (NIIM, Australia) has a study under ethics approval, using low EMF ("Rife-type / EMF") to monitor circulating tumor cells (CTCs) over time in cancer patients, with multiple sessions over months.Tumor-specific frequency modulation trial

🔬 A controlled human trial published in PMC ("Targeted treatment of cancer with radiofrequency electromagnetic fields amplitude-modulated at tumor-specific frequencies") looked at RF EMF amplitude-modulated to match frequencies identified in individual patients.

🔬Found that this treatment was safe and wel tolerated. Some patients with advanced cancer had durable responses. Also in vitro, cancer cell proliferation could be inhibited, gene expression changed, mitotio spindle disrupted.

“If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration.”

Tesla understood that everything in the universe, from the tiniest atom to the largest galaxy, is in a constant state of vibration. These vibrations, he believed, were the key to unlocking the cosmos’s deepest mysteries.

Energy, in Tesla’s view, wasn’t just about power plants and electrical currents. It was the fundamental essence of all things, constantly transforming and flowing through different states of matter and consciousness.

Frequency, he realized, was the rate at which energy vibrates. By manipulating frequencies, Tesla believed we could tap into unlimited free energy, revolutionize communication, and even influence human health and consciousness.

Tesla’s work with vibrations led to groundbreaking inventions, but also to more esoteric explorations. He spoke of the Earth as a conductor of acoustical resonance and hinted at the possibility of harnessing the planet’s natural frequencies for global communication and energy transmission.

Today, Tesla’s insights are finding new relevance in fields ranging from quantum physics to spiritual practices. His vision of a universe pulsating with vibrational energy continues to inspire scientists, inventors, and seekers alike.

How might viewing your world through Tesla’s lens of energy, frequency, and vibration transform your understanding of reality?

❌ The “Scalar Energy Generator” Myth — Debunked ❌

Devices like the “Majestic 12 Frequency Generator” are going viral again. Copper coils, sacred geometry, and promises of quantum healing… But let’s break it down from an engineering perspective — no magic, just facts.

🔻 1. “Scalar energy” isn’t real.
It’s not recognized in physics, electronics, or any peer-reviewed field. It’s a pseudoscientific term with no measurable properties or proven effects.

🔻 2. Coils ≠ function.
Without an active AC source, frequency modulation, or a closed circuit, these are just copper wires shaped in a pattern. No output. No waveform. No energy.

🔻 3. Geometry is not electricity.
Spiral layouts may look impressive, but they don’t create usable electromagnetic fields on their own. There’s no voltage, no amps, and no resonance.

🔻 4. Zero scientific evidence.
No lab validation. No controlled testing. No academic publication. Just anecdotal claims on sales pages.

🔻 5. It’s not a frequency generator.
A real frequency generator delivers precise, tunable signals. This is a static object with no electronic function.

⸻

📌 Don’t get fooled by science-sounding terms.
⚡ Real energy requires real physics — not decorative wiring.

“Dominus” in latin means “Lord” or “Master”. This word can be heard in many examples of sacred Christian Music.

Despite its noble origins, the choice of the name “Dominus” was actually inspired by Dominoes, the popular game that dates back to 13th century China and which spread out to Europe five centuries later.

In facts, the way the engine works resembles this ancient game. In Dominoes you connect the different tiles according to their value. Dominus Choir utilizes an engine which intelligently connects the different syllables by the vowels which both precede and follow the consonants.

The vowels are the glue that hold the words together. This system keeps the musicality and the realism of the original sessions intact, but also gives the user complete control over the duration of each part of the words built with the word editor.

In addition to this, Dominus features beautiful true-legato transitions for all the vowels in the library, capable of dealing with movements from the softest to the strongest dynamics, and, an intuitive polylegato engine that simply works as it should.

The sound of photosynthesis 🌿

I spent several days exploring the underwater soundscape of a pond with my hydrophone. During these recording sessions, I captured a series of sounds that reminded me of tiny bubbles popping. It makes perfect sense: in shallow water, under daylight, plants and algae release oxygen as they photosynthesize. The microbubbles that form, grow and finally detach from the leaves create brief "pops" or a faint crackling sound. 

This recording was slowed down four times to let you hear the details of the sound. 

🔊 Recorded with the ASF1-MKII hydrophone from @ambientrecording

Science has known that the body is electrical in nature. An EEG (electroencephalogram) measures the electrical activity of the brain, while an EKG/ECG (electrocardiogram) measures the electrical activity of the heart. Every thought, emotion, heartbeat, and nerve impulse involves electrical signaling.

Spiritually, many traditions have taught for centuries that we can sense another person’s energy, presence, or intention before a conversation even begins. While science has not proven that we can determine someone’s character from their electromagnetic field, it does confirm that we are not simply biochemical beings. We are also electrical beings.

Every thought and emotion has its own vibrational frequency or wave frequency. Quantum mechanics has demonstrated how a wave frequency can be altered. The shape of a wave has peaks and valleys. 

Energy waves are encoders and carriers of information with an infinite capacity for storage. When you overlay an opposite shaped wave on to an original wave, the original wave is straightened. Essentially you cancel out the characteristics of the original wave by overlaying a new exact opposite set of peaks and valleys on top of it. You change the vibrational frequency.

To change your thinking, you must adopt the belief that your perception of the world is what determines your reality. When you discover how to effectively choose your perceptions, you change your ability to cultivate happiness.

The Role of Emotions

A thought without intense emotion and feeling has no meaning, no value, and no real power to effectively engage your neural pathways. Intensity of emotion and feeling is required to take an experience and make it a solidified habit. The more emotion you engage, the more neurons you activate to form well-worn pathways. Emotions and feelings are the juice, or energy, behind your thoughts that give power to your memories, goals, hopes, and dreams.

Vibrational Frequency of Emotions

The Latin root word for emotion, emotere, literally means “energy in motion.” In and of itself, emotion is neutral. It’s neither positive nor negative. What you think of as emotion is actually the experience of energy moving through your physical body. We experience emotion as energy frequencies and their correlating physical sensations in the body such as expansion or contraction.

You can apply this to creating your goals and dreams. Like energy attracts like energy. Focus on what you want and get what you want. Begin to live from the place of already having what it is that you desire.

The next topic I wanted to explore, does using AI affect our frequency? 

This one felt very relevant to explore, especially as AI becomes such a big part of how we work and create every day.

So I connected with my higher self through my pendulum and asked. The answer is in the video.

What I find interesting is the broader question underneath this,  how does spending so much time in our heads, using technology to think for us, create for us, decide for us, affect our connection to our own intuition and inner guidance?

Hope is a low vibrational frequency ✨

The sound of moss in a tufa spring 🌱

While visiting Haute-Marne this summer, I discovered one of the region’s natural wonder: the tufière.
A tufière is a spring where water, after its underground journey, resurfaces highly charged with limestone. As it flows over mosses and algae, the limestone is deposited and forms a light rock called tuf. New moss then grows back on this stone and the cycle repeats again and again. Over time, this process creates terraced formations, with small natural dams and tiered pools.

To record the sounds, I used a geophone and a contact microphone. You can hear the subtle movements of the moss stems, followed by the flowing water in the background.

#reels #science

🌿🐦 Why does birdsong feel so calming?

You may have seen claims that birds naturally sing at 432 Hz and that this frequency directly calms the nervous system. The reality is a bit more nuanced.

Birds don’t all sing at one specific frequency, and there isn’t strong scientific evidence that 432 Hz itself has unique healing properties compared with nearby frequencies. However, research does show that natural sounds—especially birdsong—can help reduce stress and promote relaxation.

Your brain interprets birdsong as a sign that the environment is safe. When birds are singing, it often signals the absence of immediate threats, which may help activate the parasympathetic (“rest and digest”) nervous system.

The result?
✨ Slower breathing
✨ Reduced feelings of stress
✨ Lower perceived anxiety
✨ A greater sense of calm and presence

Whether it’s 432 Hz, birds outside your window, or a peaceful nature walk, taking a few moments to listen can be a simple way to support your nervous system and reconnect with the present moment.

#NervousSystemHealth #Birdsong #StressRelief #mindbody

Energy transfer becomes most efficient when the frequency of a driving force matches the natural frequency of a system — a phenomenon known as resonance. At this point, even a small input of energy can produce a large response because the system vibrates in sync with the applied force. This principle explains how a swing moves higher with properly timed pushes, how musical instruments amplify sound, and how tuning circuits in electronics work. When frequencies do not match, energy is still transferred, but much less efficiently.

Today I took a day off from editing so I could go out recording more sounds for me to edit. Well, that’s field recorders life :)! We extended our stay for another month and I will go ahead and record more ocean sounds around this beautiful island. 
A new sound library of ocean sounds from La Reunion is promised :) For the recordings I use the Zoom F6 inside the KTek bag and of course my Wildtronics Stereo Microphone set!

Is there anything more joyful and calming than the sounds of nature? 🐤 Unlike manmade noise, which can raise levels of stress hormones in our bodies and even increase our risk for cardiovascular disease, natural soundscapes set us at ease. 🌱 Recordings of natural sounds have been used to calm patients at a children’s hospital, ease the stresses of travel at an airport lounge, and relieve pain. 🎶 Birdsong has even been piped into gas-station restrooms, a move that purportedly raised customer satisfaction ratings.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
One possible reason for this: we evolved to rely on ambient sounds, particularly birdsong, as an indicator of the safety of our environment. Before a big storm, or in other dangerous moments, birds flee, and the world goes eerily quiet. ⛈ The noise of business as usual among the animals lets us know we’re free to play and explore.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
If you're looking for an auditory way to bring the outside in, try searching "Nature Sounds" on YouTube or Spotify and start streaming. 🎧 This particular video (linked in profile bio) is from Paul Dinning on YouTube —check out his channel for beautiful birdsongs. ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
